# EMITTER-GAPS.md
## D4.0: Emitter-Completeness Inventory — Self-Hosted Compiler over the LLVM Spike

**Generated by:** `compiler/entries/llvm_emit_gaps_main.mdk` (drives `llvm_emit.emitProgramGaps`
with record-and-skip ON over the REAL prelude + compiler source).
**Pipeline:** the same typed front end the LLVM emit gates use
(`desugar → elaborateDict`/`elaborateModules` route-stamping + `dict_pass` →
`lowerProgram` → **EMIT**), with the emitter's `panic` gap sites rerouted to a
record-and-continue log so a SINGLE run surfaces EVERY unlowerable construct.
**Entry:** `stdlib/runtime.mdk stdlib/core.mdk compiler/entries/all_modules_entry.mdk compiler`
(the 15-module `all_modules_entry` closure the dispatch probes use).

> Run:
> ```
> medaka run compiler/entries/llvm_emit_gaps_main.mdk \
>   stdlib/runtime.mdk stdlib/core.mdk compiler/entries/all_modules_entry.mdk compiler
> ```
> The recording flag (`gapRecordEnabled`) defaults **OFF** — every gap site still
> `panic`s on the normal path, so `diff_compiler_llvm` (129/129),
> `diff_compiler_llvm_typed` (25/25), `diff_compiler_core_ir` (20/20) and
> `diff_compiler_eval` (20/20) stay **byte-identical**. Only this probe flips it on.

This is the orthogonal axis to `DISPATCH-INVENTORY.md`: dispatch is DONE
(D0–D3b-2 — every arg-position site is `RKey`/`RDict` on the emit path), but the
spike emitter (`llvm_emit.mdk`, slices 1–8) cannot yet LOWER the construct
*shapes* the real prelude/compiler use. This sizes that closure work.

---

Two censuses: **(A)** `stdlib/core.mdk` alone through the single-file typed emit
path (`elaborateDict` + arg/return dict-passing wired — the first bootstrap
milestone), **(B)** the whole self-hosted compiler (core + 15 modules) through the
multi-module path (`elaborateModules`, as the dispatch probes use).

|17 | **extern referenced as a first-class value** (`charIs*` passed unapplied; ANY family) | 0 | ~~5~~ **0** | Emitter | ✅ **CLOSED (E17, 2026-06-08; GENERALIZED E21, 2026-06-11)** — a unicode extern (slice 14) used as a bare, unapplied value (`charPred charIsAlpha` etc. in `eval.mdk`'s `externBindings`) reached `emitVar` and fell through to `lookupVarG` → `unbound variable`. **E17** added an `isUnicodeExtern x` branch in `emitVar` routing to `emitUnicodeEtaClosure`, eta-wrapping the arity-1 unicode extern only. **E21 generalizes this to EVERY extern family** (str/num/io/abort/arrIntrinsic/arrLeaf/`arrayMakeWith`/char/strChar/unicode/adt/env/file/rng/hash/debugLit), so a point-free alias `f = stringLength` or any extern passed to a HOF lowers as a value — not just the 9 unicode externs. Mechanism: (1) the CApp head's per-family dispatch ladder (`emitApp`) is **factored into one `emitExternApplied e env fname args`**; the head path now calls `else if isAnyExtern fname && hasArgs args then emitExternApplied …` (`isAnyExtern` = OR of all family predicates + the exact-name `arrayMakeWith`) — byte-identical applied-form output (all four `diff_compiler_llvm{,_modules,_typed}`/`build` gates 172/8/37/9 unchanged). (2) `emitVar` adds `else if isAnyExtern x then emitExternEtaClosure e x`; `emitExternEtaClosure` builds a captureless static define with `externArity e x` params and a body that **REPLAYS `emitExternApplied`** on synthetic `CVar "__eta_argK__" AGlobal` args bound to `%argK` — value/applied byte-consistency by construction, correctly reproducing intrinsics with no C symbol (e.g. `stringLength`'s inline cp-count load → 4 codepoints for "café", not 5 bytes). (3) **`externArity` is sourced from the runtime signature** (`declSigOf` → arrow count): `core_ir_lower.declSigTypeNames` now also captures `DExtern` decls, and each emit driver folds `declSigTypeNames runtimeDecls` into its `installDeclSigTypes` — **no per-family arity hardcoded**. **Unicode is FOLDED IN** (the `isUnicodeExtern`→`emitUnicodeEtaClosure` special-case removed from `emitVar`; unicode now flows through `emitExternApplied`→`emitUnicodeExtern`; gates stayed byte-identical, so `emitUnicodeEtaClosure`/`emitUnicodeEtaDefine`/`unicodeExternSym` are now dead and could be pruned later). EMIT-ONLY; no new C. Every arity-N extern family is now eta-wrappable as a value; no family stayed an explicit gap. The original 5 sites — `charIsAlpha`/`charIsSpace`/`charIsUpper`/`charIsLower`/`charIsPunct` in binder `externBindings`. **B: extern-as-value 5→0; B TOTAL 23 → 18** (clean subtraction, no new downstream events). |

### Open emitter CAPABILITY gaps (NOT census events — worked around, tracked for closure)

These are emitter features the backend lacks; they don't appear in the census because the self-hosted source avoids them by convention. Listed here so they're trackable + closeable.

- **Native STACK-SAFETY for user list builders (TRMC Phase 1)** ✅ **CLOSED for cons-tail builders (2026-06-11, #56).** A cons-tail builder `f (x::xs) = g x :: f xs` (`upto`/`replicate`/`take`/hand-rolled `myMap`) lowered with the recursive call as the LAST arg of a `::`, evaluated BEFORE the cons cell is built → every frame stayed live to the base case → native SIGSEGV(139) at ~70-80k cons cells (the interpreter handles 2M+). `llvm_emit.mdk` now applies **TRMC (tail-recursion-modulo-cons / destination-passing)**: a TRMC-eligible top-level NON-DICT self-recursive define becomes an O(1)-stack loop with a mutable destination slot (`alloca ptr`) — a cons leaf allocates the cell, links it into `*dest`, advances `dest` to the cell's tail slot, recomputes the recursion args into mutable param slots, and branches back; the self-call is NEVER emitted as a `call`. Both shapes covered: single-clause guarded (`upto`, CIf-descent) and multi-clause (`myMap`, decision-tree leaf redirect via `trmcCtxRef`). Eligibility is structural + deterministic (`trmcEligible`/`isConsTail`/`trmcBodyOk`), gated to non-dict defines (`leadingDictPats == 0`). Gates 172/9/37, build 15, `diff_native_cli` 54, fixpoint **C3a/C3b YES** (the emitter's own cons builders become eligible; its IR changes but reproduces byte-for-byte). New `test/diff_native_stack.sh` (`test/stack_fixtures/`): `upto 1 2_000_000` + `myMap … (upto 1 2_000_000)` print `2000000`, exit 0, == oracle (SIGSEGV before). **Phase 2 (deferred):** dict-passed methods (`map`/`filterMap` via instances — stdlib `map (+1) [1..1M]` still overflows), `match`/`if`-arm in-position cons, and non-`::` single-constructor last-field TMC. See `compiler/TRMC-DESIGN.md`.

- **Cross-module top-level name COLLISIONS** ✅ **CLOSED (2026-06-11, universal per-module mangling).** The flat multi-module emit named each top-level Medaka function by its BARE name (`@mdk_<name>`); combining modules into one native graph (the `medaka` CLI importing `repl.mdk` + `lsp.mdk` + …) made two modules' same-named functions collide — only one `@mdk_<name>` survived and BOTH modules' callers routed to it → wrong dispatch / SIGSEGV (real case: `repl.isIdentChar : String -> Bool` vs `lsp.isIdentChar : Char -> Bool`). `compiler/private_mangle.mdk` previously de-collided only collision-TRIGGERED module-PRIVATES (safe because private refs are intra-module). It is now UNIVERSAL: EVERY top-level function in core + every module is renamed to a module-qualified unique symbol `<mid>__<name>`, and EVERY reference is rewritten IMPORT-AWARE to its resolved origin module's mangled name → collisions are impossible BY CONSTRUCTION. **Import-aware resolution** (the crux) needs no `resolve.mdk` change: the compiler namespace is FLAT (imported names are referenced bare), so per unit a `bare-name → origin-module` map is built from the unit's own `DUse` decls (`UseGroup`/`UseWild`/`UseName`/`UseAlias`) intersected with each module's EXPORTED (pub) function names, plus the implicit prelude (core's exports as `core__<name>`); a LOCAL definition shadows any import (local entries are first in the map, `lookupPM` is first-match). The pre-existing scope-aware `renameScoped` rewrite is reused verbatim (a binder named like a top-level fn still shadows it). **DTypeSig is renamed in lockstep with its DFunDef** (else the typechecker keys a constrained fn's scheme under the bare name while the def/call-sites use the mangled name → `unbound constrained fn`). **EXCLUDED:** the entry `main` (emitted as `@main`, the program entry); externs / `@mdk_<externName>` runtime C symbols (declared in runtime.mdk, never seen here; a reference to one isn't in any unit's defined-fn/import scope so it's left unchanged); constructors / interface+impl method names / dict names (their own emitter naming; not DFunDef/DLetGroup binders). EMIT-ONLY — the gates diff program OUTPUT, so a consistent rename is invisible, but a reference sent to the WRONG origin module changes output and is caught. All differential gates green (172/9/37/9; `name_collision` fixture + native CLI 53/53); `selfcompile_fixpoint` C3a + C3b YES (the emitter self-compiles with all its OWN top-level names mangled and reproduces byte-for-byte). Repro `test/llvm_fixtures_modules/name_collision/` (`alpha.tag` n+100 vs `beta.tag` n+200, both reached from `main`; oracle 302 — FAILS on the old collision-triggered mangling, emits a single `@mdk_tag` → wrong; passes after, distinct `@mdk_alpha__tag`/`@mdk_beta__tag`).
- **`CTGuard` — clause/match GUARDS.** ✅ **CLOSED (2026-06-08).** `emitTree`'s `CTGuard` arm now emits a real guard test + branch (`emitGuardedArm` re-binds the arm pattern like a leaf; `emitGuardChain` tests each `CGBool` — `True` = word 3 — branching to the `fail` subtree on failure, falling through to the body on success; mirrors `core_ir_eval.cevalGuardedBody`). `CGBool` guards + irrefutable `CGBind` (`x <- e`) work; a **refutable** `CGBind` (`Just x <- e`) is a contained `gapU` (not produced by current source). This retires the GUARD-FREE convention for match-arm `if`-guards. Fixtures `test/llvm_fixtures/guard_match_{chain,ctor}.mdk` (emit→clang→run→oracle); the native emitter still reproduces its own IR with this code (selfcompile C1 6/6). **Was** an unclosed gap that silently blanked guarded bodies to `0` under the gap-tolerant build (bit `llvm_emit.mdk`'s own `emitGroupBody`/`emitCmp` at C1).
- **`CSLet` block-let with an arbitrary irrefutable pattern (`PCon`/`PCons`/`PList`/`PAs`/nested).** ✅ **CLOSED (2026-06-11).** `emitBlock` handled `CSLet _ pat` only for `PVar`/`PWild`/`PTuple`, then fell through to `gapE "unsupported block statement (slice 1)"` — a block-level constructor-pattern let (e.g. `prop_runner.mdk:265` `let PropParam x ty = nthList params i`) blanked/aborted natively while the tree-walker (`core_ir_eval.cevalBlock`/`cBlockLet`/`cBlockLetLast`) handled it generically. Added a general mid-block arm `emitBlock e env ((CSLet _ pat ex)::rest)` (destructure via the existing `bindPattern` — the same path match-arm emission uses — into the extended env, NO failure branch since a bare `CSLet` is irrefutable by construction; a refutable surface let desugars to `CSLetElse`) plus the previously-absent last-position arm `emitBlock e env [CSLet _ pat ex]` (mirrors `cBlockLetLast`: bind + yield Unit). The specialized `PVar`/`PWild`/`PTuple` arms are kept ahead of the general one so existing output stays byte-identical. `blockTy` got the matching general arm (binds the pattern vars at `LTInt` — the value its catch-all already produced — keeping it pure, since it can't read the `<Mut>` ctor field-type table; emit-time `bindPattern` still applies precise declared scalar types). All differential gates byte-identical (172/8/37/9); `selfcompile_fixpoint` C3a + C3b YES. Repros: a `PCon`, a `PCons`, and a mid-block constructor let all build native + match the oracle. This unblocked the Phase-C Slice 3 `test` wiring (which pulls `prop_runner` into the native module graph for the first time).
- **`flushStdout` native extern** ✅ **CLOSED (2026-06-11, lsp gap 1/3).** `flushStdout : Unit -> <IO> Unit` (`stdlib/runtime.mdk:55`) was wired in the OCaml interpreter (`lib/eval.ml`) but absent from both the C runtime and the emitter's IO-extern dispatch — a native program calling it hit `lookupVarG`'s `gapE "unbound variable 'flushStdout'"`. Added `mdk_flushstdout(long long w)` to `runtime/medaka_rt.c` (ignores the Unit arg, calls `fflush(stdout)`, returns void) and: (1) added `"flushStdout"` to `isIoExtern`'s list in `llvm_emit.mdk`; (2) added an `emitIoExtern e env "flushStdout" args` arm mirroring the `putStr`/`putStrLn` arms (emit `call void @mdk_flushstdout(i64 u)`, return `("1", LTUnit)`); (3) added `declare void @mdk_flushstdout(i64)` to `emitPreamble`. All differential gates byte-identical (172/8/37/9); `selfcompile_fixpoint` C3a + C3b YES. Minimal repro: `putStr "hello"; flushStdout ()` → prints `hello` native. This is the first of 3 gaps blocking a full `medaka build compiler/entries/lsp_main.mdk`.
- **`max`/`min` over PRIMITIVE `Ord` receivers** — ✅ **CLOSED (2026-06-11).** Two distinct mechanisms hid under "the 2 max/min census events":
  - ✅ **RDict / value-position closed (gap #50, 2026-06-11)** — `max`/`min` reached through a generic `Ord a` **dict** (point-free alias `callMax = max`, a method passed to a HOF `applyOp max`, or a generic `Ord a =>` fn calling `max`/`min` internally) now lower correctly. Root cause was an arity mismatch (the dict-passed point-free define was emitted arity-`dict-only` while the call site over-applied it with the value args, dropping them; and a bare-method VALUE emitted a zero-arg dispatch then used the garbage word as a closure). Fix (emitter-only): `etaSaturateMethodBody` saturates a point-free constrained binding's under-applied method body to full arity; `emitMethodValue` eta-wraps a bare `CMethod` value into a dict-capturing dispatching closure; `emitVar` routes a bare tagged-method `CVar` to `emitMethodEtaClosure`. Verified: `callMax 3 7`→7, `callMax "a" "z"`→"z", `min` analogs, `clamp lo hi x = max lo (min hi x)`, `applyOp max 3 7`. Gates 172/9/37/9 byte-identical; fixpoint C3a/C3b YES; native CLI 54/54. See DISPATCH-GAPS-SCOPE.md §#50.
  - ✅ **arg-tag RNone-over-primitives FACET CLOSED (2026-06-11)** — `maximum`/`minimum` in core.mdk (`maximum = fold step None where step None x = Some x; step (Some m) x = Some (max m x)`). The diagnosis in the original SPLIT note ("RNone arg-tag with no primitive cell tag") was **WRONG**: the route was already correct. Both the single-file (`elaborateDict`) and module (`elaborateModules`, the build path) front-ends thread the `Ord a` dict into the inner `max m x` correctly — it lowers to `CMethod "max" (RDict $dict_maximum_1)` (verified by dumping the dict-passed Core IR with `enableArgStamp ()`). The real bug was the **#50 arity-mismatch facet recurring, but the under-applied method head buried under the `where` `CLetGroup`**: `maximum = fold step None` is point-free `(Foldable t, Ord a) =>`-constrained; `dictPass` prepends the two dict params (`$dict_maximum_0/1`) but `etaSaturateMethodBody`'s `methodBodyDeficit` only looked through a bare `CMethod`/`CApp` spine, NOT the `CLetGroup` the `where`-helper `step` wraps the `fold step None` tail in → deficit read 0 → the define came out **arity 2 (dicts only)** while the call site supplied **3 args** (2 dicts + the list container) → the value arg was dropped, the body returned the partial `fold` closure, and the caller used it as `Option Int`. **Masked at `-O0`, fatal at `-O1+`** — clang's optimizer turned the ABI mismatch into a null-dict-pointer deref (`ldr x8, [x21]` with `x21=0` inside `@mdk_core__maximum`); `medaka build` passes `-O2`, so it SIGSEGV'd (exit 139) while the un-optimized IR happened to limp through. **Fix (emitter-only, general):** `methodBodyDeficit` now recurses through `CLet`/`CLetGroup` to the tail expression, and a new `applyEtaArgsTail` threads the fresh eta args into that same tail (rebuilding the `where`/`let` wrappers so the helper bindings stay in scope). General for ANY point-free `=>`-constrained fn whose under-applied method spine sits under a `where`/`let` — not max/min-special-cased. **Verified:** `maximum [3,7,1]`→`Some 7`, `minimum [3,7,1]`→`Some 1`, `maximum ["a","z","m"]`→`Some z` (a different primitive Ord), all native==oracle; `max 3 7`/`compare 3 7`/`clamp` (concrete) unchanged. Gates 172/9/37, build **11** (added `maxprim` fixture), fixpoint C3a/C3b YES, native CLI 54/54. **Residual (separate, pre-existing — NOT this facet): ✅ NOW CLOSED — see `clamp`/compose below.** **Note:** the `llvm_emit_modules_main.mdk` "Pre-E4 no-op" driver comment is STALE — E4 (arg-position dict-passing on `elaborateModules`) HAS landed; the build path threads the dict.
- **`clamp`/compose — point-free `=>`-constrained binding whose body is a CLAM closure value (eta-saturation family, 3rd wrapper shape)** — ✅ **CLOSED (2026-06-11, gap #12).** `clamp lo hi = min hi >> max lo` desugars compose `>>`/`<<` to a `CLam` `\x -> max lo (min hi x)`. dict-passing gives the clause `[$dict, lo, hi]` (arity 3); the body is the well-formed CLam closure (its own dispatch is correct), but the define **returns it unapplied** while every call site `clamp 0 10 7` passes `$dict + 3 declared value args` (arity 4) → the 4th arg is **dropped** → garbage word out (SIGSEGV at `-O2`). This is the 3rd recurrence of the #50/#11 eta-saturation family, but the body is a **closure VALUE, not an under-applied method/dict spine** — so `methodBodyDeficit` reads 0 and the method-spine path can't fire (verified by dumping the dict-passed Core IR + the define's clause arity: `pats=[$dict,lo,hi]`, `fnArity=3` source value arity, `isLam=Y`, method-deficit `0`). **Fix (emitter-only, general):** when the method-spine deficit is 0 AND the body TAIL (through `where`/`let`) is a `CLam`, recover the shortfall from the SIGNATURE: a constrained binding's define MUST have arity `dictCount + sigValueArity` (= what `emitDictApp` passes at every call site = leading dict words ++ all declared value args), so `sigArityDeficit = leadingDictPats + fnArity − clausePats` (here `1 + 3 − 3 = 1`). Append that many fresh eta params and APPLY them to the body via the existing `applyEtaArgsTail`/`applyEtaArgs` — `CApp (CLam …) etaVar` lowers through `emitIndirect` (closure-alloc → load code_ptr → call), i.e. it applies the returned closure. The CLam-tail gate (`tailIsLam`) is essential: a bare-value point-free return is legitimate currying and eta-applying it would corrupt the body (an un-gated sig-arity deficit over-fired on an IO binding → `putStrLn takes exactly one argument` panic). General for any point-free constrained binding whose closure body falls short of its declared value arity, whatever the wrapper. **Verified:** `clamp 0 10 7`→7, `clamp 0 10 (-5)`→0, `clamp 0 10 15`→10, `clamp "c" "p" "z"`→"p" (String Ord), all native==oracle; before-fix all four emit garbage. Gates 172/9/37, build **12** (added `clampc` fixture), fixpoint C3a/C3b YES, native CLI 54/54.
- **GAP 1 — TWO-LEVEL nested instance dicts** ✅ **CLOSED (2026-06-10, Option A boxed witness).** A dict witness was a flat i64 `hashName(tag)` (rep note (j)) that dropped a non-empty `RKey _ reqs`; `ensureNoReqs` panicked on the RKey-call path but the RDict **dispatch-chain** path (`emitDispatchChain`) silently mis-lowered — calling a parametric impl fn (`mdk_impl_List_eq`) with its leading element-dict ARG missing → uninitialized `%arg` deref → **SIGSEGV** in `medaka build` for `eq [[1,2]] [[1,2]]` (element `List Int`). Option A makes every dict witness a **pointer to a heap cell** `[ i64 head_tag | i64 reqdict_0 | … ]` (via the existing `@mdk_alloc`, EMITTER-ONLY): `dictWordOfRoute`/`emitDictCell` build it (field 0 = `hashName key`, fields 1..n = the recursively-built witness words for `reqs`, in `reqs` order); `emitMethodDispatch` loads the head tag from field 0 for the `icmp` and `emitDispatchChain` prepends the matched impl's nested dicts (cell fields `0..reqCount-1`, where `reqCount = implArity − methodArity`) ahead of `argOps`. Forwarding order matches `dict_pass`'s `methRoutes ++ implRoutes` and the interpreter's structured `VDict(key, map dict_of_route reqs)` (eval.ml ~625). One-level dicts box uniformly (1-word cell), so the rep change is mechanical. `selfcompile_fixpoint` C3a + C3b re-stabilize byte-for-byte (the emitter's own dicts box, fixpoint deterministic). Fixture `test/llvm_fixtures_modules/nested_dict_dispatch/` (self-contained `Cmp (Box a) requires Cmp a` over `Box (Box Int)` — the empty-prelude analogue of `[[Int]]`; FAILS on the pre-fix emitter, passes after). **Residual:** an ADT-with-`requires` whose element is *itself* nested (`Box (List (List Int))`, the requested 2-level Box fixture) still SIGSEGVs — but that is a **typechecker route-resolution gap, NOT the emitter rep**: the multi-module `elaborateModules`/`elabModuleStamp` path stamps the Box element dict as a FLAT `RKey "List" []` (the single-module `elaborateDict` path resolves it nested correctly — `core_ir_dict_pp_main` prints `true`). With a properly-nested route the emitter handles arbitrary depth (`[[[Int]]]` direct lists work). Tracked separately; see report.
- **Container interface-impl bodies (`Eq`/`Ord`/`Debug`/`Display`) that delegate through `toList`/element-typed values** ✅ **CLOSED (2026-06-11, #54/#21 residual).** A `Debug (Map k v)` impl body `debug m = "fromList \{debug (toList m)}"` (likewise `Eq`/`Ord` via `eq/compare (toList a) (toList b)`, and Set/HashMap/Array analogues) **SIGSEGV'd at runtime** (exit 139) while the interpreter was correct. The contrast that localized it: top-level `debug (toList m)` WORKED, but `debug m` (the Map's OWN Debug impl, internally the same `debug (toList m)`) crashed. Root cause was in the typecheck route machinery (NOT the emitter rep — the GAP-1 boxed witness above is correct): the impl body's `debug (toList m)` arg-stamps `List (k, v)` → `RKey "List" [...]` and recurses into the tuple `(k, v)`, but `routeOfMono`'s nested recursion went through `implRequiresRoutesRec`/`implReqRoutes`/`reqRoute` (the RETURN-position, activeDictVar-BLIND builder) → the abstract element vars `k`/`v` (which ARE the impl's `requires Debug k, Debug v` dict params, registered in `activeDictVars`) routed `RNone` instead of `RDict $dict_debug_<slot>` → the emitted pair-`Debug` dict cell got `store i64 0` (null) inner fields → per-element `debug`/`eq`/`compare` dispatched through a null dict → SIGSEGV. **Fix (typecheck-only, two parts):** (1) `argImplRequiresRoutesRec` — an activeDictVar-AWARE analogue of `implRequiresRoutesRec` that `routeOfMono` uses, recursing via `argReqRoute`→`routeOfMono` so every nesting level forwards live impl dicts (`RDict`) rather than nulling abstract vars; SEPARATE from the return-position builder so golden routes stay byte-identical. (2) `routeRDictName` recurses into `RKey`'s nested reqs, so the `usesImplDict`/`bodyRDictNames` gate sees the now-nested `RDict $dict_<m>_<slot>` and the impl method DECLARES the `requires` dict params it references (else emitter panic `unbound dict witness '$dict_eq_0'`). General across all containers + arbitrary depth (verified `Map Int (Map Int String)`). Gates 172/9/37, build **13** (+`map_impl`), fixpoint C3a/C3b YES, native CLI 54/54. See DISPATCH-GAPS-SCOPE.md §#54/#21.
- **Point-free two-constraint `(Foldable t, Num a) =>` fns — `sum`/`product` (define dict-count vs call-site dict-count skew)** ✅ **CLOSED (2026-06-11, #55).** `sum = fold (+) 0` / `product = fold (*) 1` **SIGSEGV'd natively** (exit 139, `-O2`) while the interpreter gave `6`/`24`. **NOT an emitter bug** (and NOT the eta-saturation family above — that closes clause-params < define-arity; this is define-dict-count ≠ call-site-dict-count). Root cause in `typecheck.mdk`'s `scopeArities`: the compiler typechecker types integer literals MONOMORPHICALLY (`litType (LInt _) = TCon "Int"`), so the `0`/`1` seed pins `sum`'s `Num a` var to `Int` → `a` is NOT generalized → `sum`'s INFERRED scheme keeps only `Foldable t` (1 constraint). Every CALL site instantiates that 1-var scheme → threads ONE dict (`@mdk_core__sum(dict, list)`). But the DEFINE's dict-param count came from `dictArityOf` reading `scopeArities`, which prepended `constrainedSigArity` (the raw SYNTACTIC 2-constraint signature) ahead of the inferred entry → arity-3 define (`dict, UNUSED-2nd-dict, container`). The call passed 2 words, so the container `%arg2` read an unpassed register → garbage pointer deref → SIGSEGV at clang `-O2` (limps at `-O0`). **`elem` was the working control** because both its `Foldable t` and `Eq a` vars genuinely survive (no literal pins them) → inferred(2) == sig(2). Confirmed via panic-traces: `funConstraintsRef[core__sum] ids=1, subst=1, monos=1` at the call site; `promotedC core__sum=1 core__elem=2 | sigArity core__sum=2`. **Fix (typecheck-only, ~6 lines, `scopeArities`):** the INFERRED arity (from `crossModuleFunConstraintsRef`/`promotedC` — the surviving generalized constraint vars) now WINS over the signature-derived count: for any fn with an inferred entry, drop its `constrainedSigArity` entry; the sig count stays the fallback only for never-inferred (unreached) constrained fns. Realigns the define arity with what every call site computes from the same generalized scheme. General for ANY N-constraint point-free `=>`-fn whose body monomorphizes a subset of its constraint vars. **Verified:** `sum [1,2,3]`→6, `product [2,3,4]`→24, `sumOf = fold (+) 0; sumOf [1,2,3]`→6, all native==oracle (all 3 SIGSEGV before); controls unregressed (`elem`/`any`/`all`/`maximum`/`callMax`/`clamp`/Map-eq). (`sum` over a Float list is rejected by the typechecker in BOTH paths — the `0`-as-Int seed — a pre-existing Num-literal limitation, not this gap.) Gates 172/9/37, build **14** (+`sum_twocstr`), fixpoint C3a/C3b YES, native CLI 54/54. **Last substantive user-facing dispatch gap.** See DISPATCH-GAPS-SCOPE.md §#55.
- **Cross-module CONSTRUCTOR-NAME collision (was mis-filed as "mixed nullary/payload ADT match-lowering")** ✅ **CLOSED (2026-06-13).** The native backend intermittently corrupted a constructed ADT value — a NULLARY constructor was emitted as a boxed arity-1 cell instead of an immediate, giving a NULL field → `mdk_string_concat(NULL)` **SIGSEGV** — while the interpreter was correct. Reproduced deterministically by `medaka build compiler/entries/fuzz_gen_main.mdk` and minimal repro (`sub/lib.mdk`: `data Guardy = GBool Int | GBind Int`; `main.mdk` importing only `Other(..)`, declaring `data Gty = … | GBool | …`, mapping a `nameG` match over `[GInt, GBool, …]`). **Root cause was NOT match-lowering** (the original `fuzz_gen_main.mdk` note + this entry's prior framing were wrong): it is a cross-module ctor-name collision in the emitter's **bare-name-keyed** ctor tables. The flat multi-module emit folds EVERY module's `DData`/`DRecord`/`DNewtype` ctors into one global table (`core_ir_lower.ctorArities`, `eval.buildCtorToType`, and `llvm_emit`'s `installCtorMap`/`ctorTypes`); two ADTs in the loaded set declaring a same-named ctor with DIFFERENT arity collapse into ONE entry — a nullary ctor inherits a payload ctor's arity → boxed cell → corruption. `resolve`'s duplicate-ctor check misses it (the colliding name isn't imported into the user's scope, but the emitter loads the dependency module's `DData` into its GLOBAL tables). The interpreter is immune (structural `VCon name [fields]`, no arity/tag table — it lets two modules reuse a ctor name, the canonical/oracle behavior). **Fix (emit-only, `backend/private_mangle.mdk`):** extend the existing universal per-module name mangling — already applied to top-level FUNCTIONS — to CONSTRUCTORS. Every non-reserved ctor is renamed `<owningMid>__<ctor>` at its DEFINITION (`DData` variant / `DRecord` name / `DNewtype` con) AND at every USE site (`EVar` construct, `PCon`/`PRec` match, `ERecordCreate`, `EVariantUpdate`), import-aware (a `import M.{T(..)}` maps T's ctors to `<M>__<ctor>`; local shadows imports; core's exports as `core__<ctor>`). RESERVED ctors (`Cons`/`Nil`/`Some`/`None`/`Ok`/`Err`/`Lt`/`Eq`/`Gt`/`True`/`False` — `llvm_emit.reservedTag` + the True/False immediates) are EXCLUDED at the export step so their fixed tags survive. Collisions are then impossible by construction; the per-site owning module comes from the importing unit's scope, so construct and match always agree — matching the interpreter. `runtime/medaka_rt.c` + the reserved `MDK_TAG_*` block are UNCHANGED (the collision is among USER ctors only). **The `fuzz_gen_main.mdk` workaround is REMOVED** (projector-first `gtyTupElems`/`gtyDataName` peel restored to a direct mixed nullary/payload `Gty` match), restoring TIER=1 fuzzing. **Verified:** minimal repro + `medaka run`/`build` == oracle (`Int Bool x Float`/`5`); fuzz TIER=1 NATIVE sweep seeds 1–30 **8→0 crashes** (30/30 build crash-free); gates 180/9/37, build 21, test 9, native CLI 54, eval_dict 22/3 + batch 7/18 (#55 drift unchanged, +1 each); fixpoint C3a/C3b YES. New regression fixtures `test/llvm_fixtures_modules/ctor_collision_{nullary_payload,both_nullary,arity1_arity2,stored_list}/` (the nullary_payload + stored_list ones FAIL on the pre-fix emitter, empty output; all 4 pass after).
- **Native-emit scale failure — `unbound variable 'not'` at ~5% build rate** ✅ **CLOSED (2026-06-15).** On large well-typed programs (the full self-hosted compiler graph), the native emit pipeline intermittently produced a `unbound variable 'not'` panic at ~5% of fuzzer runs (900-run sweep: ~45 failures out of 900). The interpreter was always correct. **Root cause:** the emit drivers run `mangleUnits` (universal per-module name mangling) BEFORE `elaborateModules`, but `elaborateModules` synthesizes a bare `not` reference internally — `typecheck.mdk`'s `binopMethodApp` desugars `a != b` to `EApp (EVar "not") (EApp (EApp (EVar "==") a) b)`, inserting a bare `"not"` EVar into the Core IR. This bare name was never seen by `mangleUnits` (which operates on the source AST before elaboration), so it landed in the emitted IR as `core__not` in the `DFunDef` table but as a raw `"not"` at the call site → `lookupVarG` failed → DCE then pruned `core__not` from the live-set (since no reference reached it). **Fix (emit-only, two seams):** (1) `compiler/ir/dce.mdk` — `canonRef`: when the DCE live-set walker encounters a bare name that matches a known mangled global (`core__<name>`), it resolves to the mangled form before recording the reference, so `core__not` stays live; (2) `compiler/backend/llvm_emit.mdk` — `canonFnName`: the emit-time function-name lookup applies the same bare→mangled canonicalization before `lookupVarG`, so a synthesized `"not"` reference emits as `@mdk_core__not`. Both seams are required — DCE without emit-fix silences the crash but emits a dead-code call; emit-fix without DCE prunes the target define. **Fuzzer build rate 5%→100% clean** (900/900, `test/fuzz_diff.sh` TIER=1 native sweep). **This is also the find described as "mixed-ADT match field mis-extraction" in the AGENTS.md emitter-gaps entry** — that description was the pre-diagnosis framing; the ctor-collision (closed 2026-06-13) was a separate bug; the scale failure here is the distinct `not`-synthesis gap. Fixpoint C3a/C3b YES.

- **E23 — CONSTRAINED named function as a FIRST-CLASS HIGHER-ORDER VALUE (`CDict` in VALUE position)** ✅ **CLOSED (2026-06-28).** A `=>`-constrained NAMED function passed UNAPPLIED to a higher-order combinator — `filter myParity [1,2,3,4]`, `map inc xs` (`inc : Num a => a -> a`), a user HOF `apply f y = f y; apply isEv 4` — failed to BUILD with `unsupported Core IR node CDict (dictionary)` while `run` (interpreter) was always correct. **NOTE this is the VALUE facet distinct from gap #18/E18 above:** E18 scope-guarded a constrained-fn name *shadowed by a local* so it stays a plain `EVar` (never a `CDict`); E23 is the GENUINE constrained-fn occurrence used as a value, which legitimately lowers to `CDict name routes` (one Route per `=>` constraint, from `EDictAt`, `core_ir_lower.mdk`). The emitter handled `CDict` only in CALL/head position (`emitApp`→`emitDictApp`, the under-application/dict-prepend path); a bare `CDict` reaching `emitExpr` in operand position fell through to the `emitTree`/`emitExpr` catch-all (`gapE … ctag (CDict _ _)`) and panicked. A LAMBDA arg (`map (x => x+1) xs`) was unaffected — it lowers to `CLam`, emitted as a closure — so the gap was specifically a NAMED constrained fn used as a value. **Root cause:** no `emitExpr` arm for `CDict` in value position. **Fix (emit-only, `compiler/backend/llvm_emit.mdk`):** new `emitExpr e env (CDict name routes) = emitDictValue …`, mirroring the existing `emitMethodValue` (CMethod-as-value) path: `emitDictValue` builds the route dict words AT the value site (`dictWordsOf`, same as the call path), captures them in a closure cell via `emitClosureAllocA`, and emits a forwarder `emitDictValDefine` that reloads the dict words from `%clos` fields 1..nDicts (`loadPapCaptures`, the E22 PAP machinery) and prepends them ahead of the `%argK` value words in a DIRECT `@mdk_<name>(dicts.., args..)` call — the same leading-dict-then-value-arg order `emitDictApp`/dict_pass use at every saturated site. The closure VALUE has the exact ABI HOFs expect (field 0 = code_ptr, fields 1.. = captured dicts), the lambda path is the reference. Value arity = `fnArity e name − listLen routes` (matching `methodBodyDeficit`'s CDict deficit); a nullary value arity degenerates to the saturated `emitDictApp … []`. **Reused** the E22/PAP capture+forward shape (`loadPapCaptures`, `etaArgOps`, `etaParamDecls`, `emitClosureAllocA`), NOT `emitMethodPap` directly (that path dispatches a method; here the forwarder is a plain `@mdk_<name>` call). **Verified run==build:** `filter myParity [1,2,3,4]`→`[2,4]`, `map inc [1,2,3]`→`[2,3,4]`, `apply isEv 4`→`True`, all native build == interpreter, exit 0; the `higher_order` diff_fixtures eval canary now also BUILDS (run==build). Fixpoint C3a/C3b YES. New fixture `test/llvm_fixtures/clo_hof_constrained.mdk` (the `apply isEv 4` repro — produces a real `@mdk_dictval_*` closure; FAILS on the pre-fix emitter). **wasm residual:** `compiler/backend/wasm_emit.mdk`'s `emitRefExpr` already has a `CDict name routes => emitDictRef … []` value arm, but — exactly like its sibling `CMethod … []` arm — it emits a truncated saturated `call $name` with only the dict words and no value args (arity-mismatched / no closure capture), a PRE-EXISTING wasm limitation shared with the never-fixed CMethod-value case. A proper wasm fix needs a dict-capturing wasm closure (mirroring `emitClosure`) and is out of scope for this LLVM-focused fix; reported as residual.
  - **wasm residual NOW CLOSED (2026-06-28).** `wasm_emit.mdk`'s `emitRefExpr` value arms now route a bare `CMethod`/`CDict` (operand position) through new `emitMethodValueRef`/`emitDictValueRef` instead of the truncated `emitMethodRef/emitDictRef … []`. Both ETA-EXPAND the occurrence to a synthetic lambda `\a0..a(k-1) -> head a0..a(k-1)` and lower it via the existing W4 `emitClosure` — the universal closure path that (a) captures the route's forwarded dict params into the `$clos` `$env` (because `freeVarsExpr`/`freeVarsRoute` already surface RDict/RDictFwd dict-param names), and (b) re-emits the SATURATED dispatch in the lifted body via the CALL-position `emitMethodRef`/`emitDictRef` (`dicts ++ args ++ call`). The result is a `$clos` struct (code_ptr, arity, captured-dict `$env`) with the exact W4 ABI WasmGC HOFs expect — the working lambda path IS the reference, mirroring the LLVM `emitDictValue`/`emitMethodValue`. CDict value arity = `progFnArity − listLen routes`; CMethod value arity = the method's USER arity via `methodValArity` (the installed iface table `methodArityOf`, falling back to the method's impl clause arity for the prelude-free W5/typed path, which never installs that table). Verified run==build==wasm (node v24): the new `test/wasm/fixtures_typed/disp_hof_value.mdk` (`applyTo eqSelf 5` = CDict value, `apply2 myeq 3 3` = CMethod value → 7) is byte-identical native build vs wasm; `diff_wasm_typed` 7/7, `diff_wasm` 139/0, LLVM `diff_compiler_*` 68/0, fixpoint C3a/C3b YES. The real-prelude `filter myParity [1,2,3,4]` repro now emits a CORRECT dict-capturing eta-closure (`$mdk_lam*` pushing the dict witness + value arg + `call $myParity`), but the WHOLE module still can't VALIDATE under wasm because of the SEPARATE, pre-existing `diff_wasm_modules` "real-prelude point-free impl arity" GAP (foldMap-class impls emit an under-applied `fold step empty` missing the container arg) — that gap is unrelated to the value-position fix and blocks the modules path regardless.

- **E24 — HOF PARAMETER whose name SHADOWS an interface method (`pickEq eq x y = eq x y`)** ✅ **CLOSED (2026-06-28; LLVM).** A higher-order function whose PARAMETER (or any local `let`-binding) is named like an interface method — `eq`, `compare`, `cmp`, `show`, `map`, … — and is then APPLIED in the body (`eq x y`) failed to BUILD with the slice-7 gap `arg-tag dispatch on impl type that owns no constructors (slice 7: primitive receiver carries no cell tag)`, while `medaka run` was ALWAYS correct (eval treats the param as the local it is). This is the **emitter-side analogue of gap #13/E15** (and its #18/E18 sibling): E15/E18 scope-guarded the FRONT-END `prePassDictArg`/`rewriteArgScoped` rewrite so a method-named local stays a plain `EVar`/`EVarFrom` (never an `EMethodAt`/`EDictAt`) — but `compiler/backend/llvm_emit.mdk`'s `emitApp` then INDEPENDENTLY mis-routed that plain `CVar "eq"` head: it checked `isImplMethod e "eq"` (true — `eq` is an interface method name) BEFORE recognising the head as a local, falling to `emitMethodArgDispatch` → arg-tag dispatch over `eq`'s impl groups (incl. PRIMITIVE Int/String/Char/Bool that own no runtime ctor cell tag) → `emitTagMatch []` slice-7. The genuine constrained-value-as-HOF-arg path (`pickEq f x y = f x y; pickEq (==) …` with a NON-method param name) already worked via E22/E23 `emitMethodValue`/`emitDictValue` — so the gap was SPECIFICALLY the method-named local in HEAD position. **Root cause:** `emitApp`'s CVar arm checked ctor/extern/known-fn/impl-method dispatch WITHOUT a leading `isLocal env fname` guard; the VALUE-position twin `emitVar` already prioritised `isLocal` first. **Fix (emit-only, `compiler/backend/llvm_emit.mdk`):** add `if isLocal env fname then emitIndirect e env hd args` as the first check after the `Ref`/`setRef` syntactic forms — a locally-bound name shadows any same-named ctor / known fn / method / extern and applies as an INDIRECT closure call. Mirrors `emitVar`'s isLocal-first priority. **Verified run==build:** `pickEq eq x y = eq x y; pickEq (==) 2 3`→`False`, the `compare`/`show`/`map`-named-param twins, and String/List receivers, all native build == interpreter, exit 0. New fixture `test/build_diff_fixtures/hof_shadows_method.mdk`. All 68 `diff_compiler_*` + 41 `build` gates pass; wasm gates unaffected (139/0, 7/7); fixpoint C3a/C3b YES; seed re-minted; cold `bootstrap_from_seed` PASS. **wasm residual:** the WasmGC backend has a PARALLEL but DISTINCT manifestation (`medaka build --target wasm` of the same repro fails wasm VALIDATION with `type mismatch: expected (ref eq) but nothing on stack`); `wasm_emit.mdk`'s `emitAppRef` already routes an unknown head to `emitIndirectApp`, so the wasm failure is NOT a clean mirror of this one-line LLVM guard and needs its own diagnosis — reported as a follow-up (the LLVM backend is canonical).
  - **wasm residual NOW CLOSED (2026-06-29, `d143972`, emitter-only).** DIAGNOSE-FIRST overturned the filed framing: the literal `pickEq (==) 2 3` repro's `--target wasm` failure (`expected (ref eq) but nothing on stack` at `$mdk_impl_List_foldMap`) is NOT a pickEq bug — it is the SEPARATE pre-existing **#24** real-prelude point-free impl-arity gap, which blocks EVERY real-prelude modules-path program (even `main = 5` fails identically). The GENUINE E24-peer bug reproduces cleanly in the prelude-free path when a method-named HOF param ALSO collides with a top-level fn name and is applied in the body: `eq a b = 999; pickEq eq x y = eq x y; main = pickEq (a b => a+b) 2 3` → oracle/LLVM `5`, but wasm emitted `return_call $eq` (the wrong top-level callee) → silent miscompile `999`. **Root cause (two consistent sides, both `compiler/backend/wasm_emit.mdk`):** (1) emit — `emitAppRef`/`emitAppTail` CVar arms resolved a head to a direct `call $<toplevel>`/ctor WITHOUT first checking `contains f0 env`, so a shadowing local lost to the same-named top-level fn; (2) analysis — the closure-usage scan (`exprUsesClosures`/`appUsesClosures`, now threading a `locals` set seeded from clause params + let/lambda binders; `stmtUsesClosures`→`blockUsesClosures`) didn't count a shadowing-local head as a closure use, so the `$argarr`/`$codety`/`$clos` types weren't declared though emit used them → a parse failure. **Fix:** both sides now check `contains … env` FIRST (mirrors `emitVarRefPlain`'s isLocal-first priority and the LLVM `isLocal` guard) → a locally-bound head applies indirectly (`return_call $mdk_apply`). New fixture `test/wasm/fixtures_typed/disp_hof_shadows_method.mdk` (typed gate computes the native-compiled oracle on the fly, no golden file). Gates: `diff_wasm` 139/0, `diff_wasm_typed` 8/0, `diff_wasm_modules` 0/20-gap/0-fail (unchanged — the 20 gaps are #24), LLVM `diff_compiler_llvm` 186/0 (wasm_emit.mdk outside the LLVM path); no seed re-mint (outside the self-compile graph).

- **#23 / slice-7 SIGTRAP — constrained HOF whose own Num constraint comes from internal literals (`app2 f = f 2 3; main = println (app2 (==))`)** ✅ **CLOSED (2026-06-28, typecheck-only).** A user HOF generalized to `(Num a, Num b) => …` from literals applied INSIDE its body (`app2 f = f 2 3`, `useIt h = h 2 3`), called with a function that ties those Num vars to ANOTHER class and yields a concrete result — `app2 (==)` / `useIt eqBoth` (the var is `Num`+`Eq`, the result `Bool`) — BUILT but the binary exited 133 (SIGTRAP), while `medaka run` printed the correct value (`False`). **run≠build silent miscompile.** **ACTUAL root cause (the prior session's "defaulting misses the var" framing was a symptom, NOT the root — verified empirically by dumping the emit-path Core IR: `main` lowered to `(CDict "app2" (RNone RNone))`, two NULL dict words → `inttoptr 0; load` → SIGTRAP):** the inferred constraint's **iface name was LOST**. `ifaceForConstraintId` (which `registerInferredFor` uses to fill `funConstraintIfacesRef`) only recovers the iface from a `pendingImplObligation` whose `occ` is a **bare `TVar id`**, but a Num constraint induced by a `fromInt`/method site records a **COMPOUND occ** (the whole method type) — so it returned `""`. Empty iface ⇒ (a) `routeUndeterminedTop ""` → `RNone` (silent null dict, NOT the `AmbiguousImpl` error a real `"Num"` iface would raise), and (b) `recordCallObligations` SKIPS empty-iface slots, so the defaulting pass never saw the var as a Num candidate. **Fix (two parts, `compiler/types/typecheck.mdk`):** **(A) `ifaceForInferredId`** — when `ifaceForConstraintId` returns `""`, fall back to `schemeObligationsRef` (which pairs each scheme var with its iface via `dispatchMonosOf`, robust to compound occs — covers a fn whose own body has the literal, `app2`), then to a `pendingDictApps` slot scan (recovers a constraint **forwarded** from a constrained callee, `useApp g = app2 g`, whose iface never lands in impl-obligations/scheme-obligations). Fallback-only ⇒ existing non-`""` routes stay byte-identical. **(B) `processSCC` Num-defaulting** now also feeds the group's `pendingCallObligations`/`pendingDictApps` Num deltas (`numCallObls`/`numDictObls`) into `defaultGroupNum`/`defaultEachMember`, so once (A) makes the obligation identifiable as Num, the ambiguous var grounds to Int **before** route resolution (`routeOfMonoTop` → `headTyconMono Some "Int"` → `RKey "Int"`, a real dict). The `not arg-reachable` placeholder filter still protects a genuinely-polymorphic call-constrained var (`useApp` at a Float-pinned result stays Float; `sumPair 1.5 2.5` → 4.0). **Verified:** `app2 (==)`/`useIt eqBoth`/`app2 (x y => x == y)`/`useApp (==)` all build AND run==build (`False`, exit 0); controls (`app2 addBoth`→5, `twice 2`→4, `sumPair` Float/Int, result-pinned-Float-not-forced-to-Int, `if app2 (==)` branch) unregressed; `selfcompile_fixpoint` C3a/C3b YES; cold `bootstrap_from_seed` PASS (seed re-minted); full `diff_compiler_*` 68/68 (build gate 42/42); `medaka test stdlib/core.mdk` 9/9 + `list.mdk` 12/12 (neq-hang canary clean). Regression fixture `test/build_diff_fixtures/num_hof_ambig.mdk`. The prior two failed attempts targeted the defaulting candidate set alone but missed that the var was unidentifiable-as-Num (empty iface) — (A) was the missing half.

\* core's `__hashRaw` (×5), `debugStringLit` (×1), `debugCharLit` (×1) were
references to runtime/core primitives the spike's extern catalog didn't carry —
the core-scale instance of gap #7.  **CLOSED 2026-06-08**: `__hashRaw` was replaced
by five SPECIFIED per-type hashers (`hashInt`/`hashString`/`hashChar`/`hashBool`/
`hashFloat`, FNV-1a + SplitMix64-finalizer mix, byte-identical oracle/native — the
RNG playbook), and all seven externs are now wired into `llvm_emit.mdk`'s extern
catalog + `emitPreamble` declares (with `mdk_hash_*`/`mdk_debug_*_lit` in
`runtime/medaka_rt.c`).  Census A's 7 → 0 (total A 30 → 23); census B 2677 → 2660
(the extern refs incl. `lexer.mdk`'s 10 `debugStringLit` calls).  The remaining
core-`#7` count is 0 — core has no genuine top-level value / `Ref` global refs.

\*\*\* **E1b (2026-06-08):** top-level non-`main` non-fn value / mutable `Ref`
bindings now emit as LLVM **globals** (`@mdk_g_<name>`) initialised once in an
`@main` prologue (source order), referenceable from any `define` (see detail #7).
Census B's genuine value-global references — **781 → 146** "unbound variable"
events (whole TOTAL **2660 → 2025**) — drop to 0; the 146 residual are
PRec/PList/PAs pattern-cascade + extern-fn-as-value, NOT #7. **PList/PAs
pattern-cascade now CLOSED (E6, 2026-06-08)** — see gap #4 detail below;
the residual unbound-vars from PList/PAs patterns vanish, B TOTAL 2025→1927.
MULTI-MODULE
cross-module init order is deferred to D4 (census B flattens to one program, so it
measures the single-program capability).

† Closing gap #10 (E5) makes the **TOTAL tick UP** (A 31→34, B 3001→3008 at
measurement time), not down: the old emitter recorded ONE gap per unsupported
`let`-group and skipped its body, so the worker bodies' own residue was hidden.
Now the `where`-workers emit, and the emitter descends INTO them — exposing the
guard residue (`otherwise` #8, `__fallthrough__` #9) and non-var patterns (#4)
that `find`/`count`/`maximum`/`minimum` carry. `arbitraryList`'s worker emits
cleanly (no guards). All revealed reasons are pre-existing categories; the
*structural* `let`-group gap is gone (5→0 in both censuses, no mutual-recursion
site left).

‡ Closing gap #11's impl-body half (E9) makes the **B TOTAL tick UP** (1924→1942),
same mechanism as the † note: the `Debug`/`Display`/`Hashable (List a)` impl bodies
previously gapped EARLY at the unbound `$dict_<m>_0` witness and were skipped, so
their forwarded-helper residue was hidden. Now `debug`/`display`/`hash`@List get
their dict param and the emitter descends INTO them + the forwarded helpers
(`debugListItems`/`displayListItems`), exposing those helpers' OWN pre-existing
arg-tag residue (gap #2 "primitive receiver carries no cell tag" — routing-not-wired,
closes when D3b arg-pos dict-passing ports to `elaborateModules`). All revealed
reasons are pre-existing categories; **A: 3→0** (the milestone metric) is a clean
drop. The A core total is now **8** across 3 reasons: 6 `Arbitrary` unit-head-switch
events (gap #12 — `arbitraryString`, `arbitrary@Int`, `arbitrary@Bool`, …;
excludable from bootstrap) + 1 `max` + 1 `min` arg-tag-fallback event (gap #13
family — `maximum`/`minimum`'s unresolved RNone). core.mdk is one routing-port and
the excludable `Arbitrary` impl away from emitting end-to-end.

§ **E4 (2026-06-08): port D3b ARG-position dict-passing onto `elaborateModules`**
(gated behind `emitArgStampPasses`, so the golden module drivers stay byte-identical).
Pre-E4 `elaborateModules` stamped only RETURN-position routes; every arg-position
method occurrence stayed `RNone` → arg-tag → census-B gap #2 on a primitive
receiver. E4 mirrors `elaborateDict` per module: under `emitArgStampPasses` it sets
`implInferEnabled True`, sets `argDispatchIdxRef = argDispatchIndices` over the
whole module graph, prePasses each module with `prePassDictArg` (the arg-pos method
set → EMethodAt), and `elabModuleStamp` calls `resolveArgStamps` (impl table
accumulated in dependency order: core + earlier modules + this module) right after
`resolveSites`, while `activeDictVars`/the tyvar cells are still this-module-bound.
**Gap #2: 1399 → 344** (top-level constrained-fn sites — `neq`/`print`/`println`/
`sum`/`elem`/`debugListItems`/… — now stamp `RKey <head>` or `RDict <dict>`; the
344 residual are IMPL-body sites `eq@List`/`eq@Option`/`eq@Result`, which
`checkModuleFull` does not infer). **B TOTAL: 1942 → 888.** A handful of new
`unbound dict witness $dict_<fn>_0` events (#11 family) surface because the now-
resolved constrained fns need their dict threaded at emit — emitter-side, not a
routing regression. New module fixture `prim_arg_dispatch` (interface+impls in the
entry, impl AT primitive `Int` + at an imported ADT `Widget`, dispatched in arg
position → 113) byte-identical native/oracle.

\*\*\*\*\* The B census #2 was **540** when first measured; subsequent emit closures
(E6/E9) made the emitter descend into MORE bodies (the † / ‡ tick-up mechanism),
exposing more pre-existing arg-tag residue → **1399** by the E4 baseline. E4 then
drops it to **344**.

¶ **E10 (2026-06-08): runtime header-dispatch fallback for `++`** closes census-B
gap #3. ~328 whole-compiler `++` sites (`scanTriple`/`splitLines` string-builders)
have an operand whose static LTy the emitter can't recover, so E2a's
`LTStr`/`LTCon` static dispatch doesn't apply and `emitBin`'s otherwise arm
`gapE`d. E10 emits `call i64 @mdk_append` there instead — a C helper
(`runtime/medaka_rt.c`) that header-dispatches at runtime (odd ⇒ Nil/list; even
header==`MDK_STR_TAG` ⇒ string; else ⇒ Cons/list) to the existing
`mdk_string_append`/`mdk_list_append`. The result VALUE is always correct; the
statically-unknowable result LTy defaults to `LTStr` (gap is String-builder-
dominated; if a value is a List the append is still correct, only a downstream
static string-op/print would mis-select — but `emitPrint LTCon` already panics on
lists, and fixtures project String results through `stringLength`). **Gap #3: 328
→ 0. B TOTAL: 888 → 560.** Fixture `concat_unknown` (oracle 6) byte-identical
native/oracle; E2a's static `LTStr`/`LTCon` dispatch untouched.

The **(A) core total of 57** spans only **~11 gap kinds**; **(B) whole-compiler
2248** spans 151 distinct reasons, but 88% collapse into the 7 categories above
the fold (rows 1–7). The long tail of (B) is row #7 enumerated per-name (one
"unbound `X`" reason per distinct global binding).

**Relation to the D3b-2 deferred seeds** (commit `b48637e`): the note named
"multi-clause / point-free / operator-section top-level fns" + "1 RNone `empty`".
This inventory confirms **multi-clause** as gap #1 (the dominant wall).
**Point-free / operator-section top-level bindings desugar cleanly** to
`CLam`/`CApp` *before* Core IR, so they are NOT a distinct emitter gap — they emit
as closures standalone, and only surface (as gap #7) when referenced as a
top-level *value* from another function. The **`empty` RNone** is the
return-position default-body seed already tracked in `DISPATCH-INVENTORY.md`; on
this emit path it lands in the arg-tag/"no impl groups" bucket (#2/#13 family),
not a new shape.

---

### PER-GAP DETAIL

#### 1 — multi-clause top-level function (A:16, B:732) — the dominant wall — ✅ CLOSED (E1a, 2026-06-07)
`emitFn` accepted a single variable-pattern clause; a `name p1 … = …` /
`name p2 … = …` group panicked before its body is even reached.
- e.g. `thenCmp`, `debugListItems`, `displayListItems` (core); virtually every
  recursive worker in `parser.mdk`/`typecheck.mdk`/`eval.mdk` (whole).
- **Emitter gap.** The machinery already exists for *impl* methods
  (`emitGroupBody` coalesces per-clause patterns into one `compileTree`
  decision tree); the fix is to route ordinary multi-clause fn-binds through the
  same clause-coalescing + decision-tree path instead of the single-clause
  `emitFnClause`. This is THE prerequisite to emitting any real module.
- **✅ CLOSED — E1a (2026-06-07).** `emitFn` now routes any non-`allPVar` /
  multi-clause fn-bind to `emitMultiClauseFn`, which calls the shared
  `emitClauseTree` (factored out of `emitGroupBody`) — one path for impl methods
  AND top-level fns. Signature inference extended to multi-clause bindings
  (`inferMultiSig`), so the synthetic `aK` scrutinee params are typed from the
  fn's sig (`implParamEnvTyped`) and `fnRetTy` reports the right result type for
  `main`'s print selection. **Also closes the fn half of #4** (the decision tree
  binds patterned params via `bindPattern`). Re-measured over core.mdk: gap #1
  16→**0**, #4-fn 4→**0**; whole-compiler #1 732→**0**. core total 57→44 (the
  wall no longer masks the now-reachable bodies' downstream value-shape gaps).

#### 2 — arg-tag dispatch on a primitive receiver (A:0, B:1399→344) — ✅ PARTIAL (E4, 2026-06-08)
`emitMethodArgDispatch`'s tag chain reaches `emitTagMatch […]` on an impl type
that owns no constructors (an `Int`/`String`/… immediate carries no cell tag).
- **Routing, not emitter.** Section **A is 0** because the single-file path wires
  D3b's arg-position dict-passing (`preludeArgPosDictNames` → `RDict`). Section B
  used `elaborateModules`, which did NOT apply that wiring, so `neq`/`print`/
  `println`/`sum`/`elem`/… fell back to `RNone`→arg-tag and hit a primitive.
- **✅ PARTIAL — E4 (2026-06-08).** Ported the proven D3b arg-position selector onto
  `elaborateModules`, GATED behind `emitArgStampPasses` (so the golden module/typed/
  eval drivers stay byte-identical). Per module, under the gate: `implInferEnabled
  True`; `argDispatchIdxRef = argDispatchIndices` over the whole module graph;
  `prePassDictArg` rewrites arg-position method occurrences to EMethodAt; and
  `elabModuleStamp` calls `resolveArgStamps` (impl table = core + earlier modules +
  this module, accumulated in dependency order) right after `resolveSites`, while
  `activeDictVars` + the tyvar cells are still this-module-bound. Zero emitter-side
  work. **B: 1399 → 344** (top-level constrained-fn sites resolve; the 344 residual
  are IMPL-body sites — `eq@List`/`eq@Option`/`eq@Result` — which the modules path's
  `checkModuleFull` does not infer, no impl-body inference). New module fixture
  `prim_arg_dispatch` exercises a primitive-`Int` + imported-ADT arg-position
  receiver (→113, byte-identical). The residual impl-body half is a later step
  (add impl-body inference to the multi-module path, mirroring `checkProgramSeeded`).

#### 3 / 6 — `++` and `::` survive lowering as `CBinPrim` (A:16, B:269) — ✅ CLOSED (E2a, 2026-06-07)
`core_ir.mdk` deliberately keeps `::` and `++` as `CBinPrim` (its own comment);
`emitBin` only knows arithmetic + comparison, so both fall to the gap.
- `++`: e.g. `impl append@List/@String`, `impl debug@List`, `scanStr*` (string
  building). `::`: `impl map@List`, `impl filterMap@List`, scanner cons loops.
- **Emitter gap.** `::` → a 2-field `Cons` cell alloc (the slice-3 ctor path,
  already present). `++` → dispatch on operand type: `String` to the
  `@mdk_string_concat` extern (present), `List` to an append loop. Small, with
  most primitives already emitted for other slices.
- **✅ CLOSED — E2a (2026-06-07).** `emitBin` now handles `"::"` (→ `emitCtorAlloc e "Cons" [lw, rw]`)
  and `"++"` (→ `mdk_string_append` for `LTStr`, `mdk_list_append` for `LTCon`). Two new C
  helpers in `runtime/medaka_rt.c`; declared in `emitPreamble`. `typeOf` extended: `CBinPrim
  "::"` → `LTCon`. Re-measured: **`::` A:2→0, B:90→0**; **`++` A:14→4** (the 4 remaining
  events are `impl append@List`, `impl append@String`, `impl ap@List`, `impl andThen@List` —
  params / fn-call results both unknown type, so `paramUseTy` falls back to `LTInt` and
  the type-dispatch arm can't fire).
  core total: 57→44 (E1a) → **32 (E2a)**. New fixtures: `concat_str`, `cons_list`,
  `concat_list`, `concat_str_unicode` — all byte-identical (140/140 untyped, 25/25 typed).
- **✅ PARTIAL — E2c (2026-06-08): static impl-param typing closes the `append` pair.**
  `append xs ys = xs ++ ys` in `impl Semigroup (List a)` and `impl Semigroup String` were
  unreachable because `emitGroupBody` passed `implParamEnv` (all `LTInt`) to `emitClauseTree`,
  which in turn used `paramUseTy` to type-infer `xs`/`ys` — but peer params default to
  `LTInt`, so `++` hit the gap.  Fix: `emitGroup` now extracts the `positions` list from the
  `ImplGroup` and passes it with the `tag` into `emitGroupBody`.  `emitGroupBody` gains a
  fast path for single-clause all-PVar impls: `paramEnvByPos` builds `xs → (%arg0, LTCon)`
  / `ys → (%arg1, LTCon)` (for `List`) or `LTStr` (for `String`) directly from `tagToLTy tag`
  at the receiver positions, bypassing the `emitClauseTree` tuple-scrutinee indirection that
  lost the type.  For non-all-PVar clauses (constructor patterns: `eq Nil Nil = True`, etc.)
  the general `emitClauseTree` path is used unchanged — and `implParamEnvByPos` types the
  synthetic `aK` scrutinee params correctly there too.  `tagToLTy` maps `"String"→LTStr`,
  `"Int"→LTInt`, `"Float"→LTFloat`, `"Bool"→LTBool`, `"Char"→LTChar`, everything else→`LTCon`.
  **A: 4→2** (`append@List`, `append@String` fixed; `ap@List` and `andThen@List` remain — their
  `++` operands are fn-call RESULTS (`map f xs`, `f x`), not direct params, so param typing
  alone can't resolve them).  **B: 1927→1925**.  New typed fixtures: `impl_append_str` (→6),
  `impl_append_list` (→10) — both byte-identical (27/27 typed gate).  core total: 17→**15**.
- **✅ FULLY CLOSED — E7 (2026-06-08): method-call RETURN-TYPE inference closes the `ap`/`andThen` pair.**
  The last 2 A events were `impl ap@List` (`map f xs ++ ap fs xs`) and `impl andThen@List`
  (`f x ++ andThen xs f`): the `++` LEFT operand is a CALL RESULT, not a param, so E2c's
  static impl-param typing can't reach it.  The emitter now statically types method-call
  results: per **(interface, method)** it computes whether the method's RESULT type mentions
  the interface/self type var (`returnsSelfTable`, in `core_ir_lower.mdk` — the result-side
  analogue of `dispatchPositionsOf`'s per-arg `tyMentions`), and whether any ARG is a function
  whose result mentions self (`selfFnParamTable`).  Both tables are computed from the iface
  decls in `core_ir_lower` and installed into the emitter via module-level Refs
  (`installReturnsSelf`/`installSelfFnParams`, no `CProgram` shape change → goldens
  byte-identical).
    - `ap@List`: `map f xs` is a CMethod RKey-dispatched at `List`; `map` returns self, so
      `emitMethod` types its result `tagToLTy "List" = LTCon` (was `LTInt`).  The recursive
      `ap fs xs` (RKey List, `ap` returns self) likewise types `LTCon`.  `emitBin`'s existing
      `++` LTCon path then picks `mdk_list_append` — no `emitBin` change.
    - `andThen@List`: the `++` LEFT operand `f x` is an INDIRECT call through callback param
      `f : a -> m b` (a function returning self).  `emitGroup` sets a current-impl context
      (tag + the names of self-returning-fn params) so `emitIndirect` types `f x` as the
      container; the recursive `andThen xs f` is an RKey-List returns-self method call typed
      `LTCon`.
  **A: 2→0** (the `++` reason is gone from census A entirely).  **B: 334→333** (the
  whole-compiler `andThen@List` instance also closes; the 333 residual are unrelated string-
  builder `++` in `scanTriple`/`splitLines`, an E4/E5-tail category).  New typed fixtures:
  `impl_ap_list` (→66, Applicative `ap` summed), `impl_andthen_list` (→206, monadic bind
  summed) — both byte-identical native/oracle (29/29 typed gate).  core total: 15→**13**.

#### 4 / 5 — non-variable parameter patterns, fn + lambda (A:5, B:258) — fn ✅ CLOSED (E1a), lambda ✅ CLOSED (E2b)
`emitFnClause`/`emitLamGo` required every parameter to be a plain `PVar`;
`fst (a,b)=a`, `snd`, `const`, and destructuring lambdas (`sepThen`, `lamTail`…)
carry tuple/constructor param patterns.
- **Disposition was desugar/lower; realised emitter-side** (no Core IR rewrite —
  goldens stay byte-identical). Both halves route the single clause through the
  shared `emitClauseTree` decision tree (factored from the impl-method path), where
  a tuple/constructor param binds its vars via `bindPattern` in the leaf.
- **fn half ✅ CLOSED — E1a (2026-06-07).** `emitFn` routes a non-`allPVar` clause
  to `emitMultiClauseFn` → `emitClauseTree`. core 4→0, whole 150→0.
- **lambda half ✅ CLOSED — E2b (2026-06-08).** `emitLam`/`emitRecLam` route a
  non-`allPVar` `CLam` to `emitPatLamDefine`, which runs `emitClauseTree` over the
  `%argK` params *inside the lifted-lambda frame* (after `%clos`/capture binding —
  the recursive case prepends `f -> %clos` so the self-call re-enters the same
  cell). Capture computation is unchanged: `patVarNames` already collects nested
  pattern vars, so patterned param vars are correctly excluded from the captured
  set. **core 1→0, whole 185→0** (185, not the earlier 108 — E1a unblocked more
  reachable bodies that carry destructuring lambdas). core total 32→**31**.
  Fixtures: `lam_tuple_param` (applied tuple-param lambda), `lam_ctor_param`
  (`Some`-style constructor-param lambda), `lam_rec_tuple` (recursive `let … in`
  lambda with a tuple param → `emitRecLam`). All byte-identical (143/25/20/20).
- **PAs/PList residue in `bindPattern` ✅ CLOSED — E6 (2026-06-08).** After E1a/E2b
  the decision-tree SWITCH side was correct (`canonPat` normalises both: `PAs _ p →
  canonPat p` transparent; `PList (h::r) → PCon consName [h, PList r]`), but
  `bindPattern`'s variable-extraction still fell to the gap on `PAs` and non-empty
  `PList`.  Two new arms added (before the catch-all):
  - `bindPattern e env (PAs x p) v body` — bind `x` to whole value `v` (same
    `paramUseTy`/`LTInt` default as `PVar`), then recurse into `p` with the same `v`:
    `(x,(v,ty)) :: bindPattern e env2 p v body`.
  - `bindPattern e env (PList (p::ps)) v body` — `v` is a proved Cons cell; delegate
    to `bindFields e env [p, PList ps] v body 0`, which loads field 0 for `p` and
    field 1 for the tail `PList ps`; the recursion bottoms out at the existing
    `PList [] → env` arm.
  **core #4 residue: 4→0.  core TOTAL: 23→17.  B TOTAL: 2025→1927.**
  Fixtures (all byte-identical oracle/native, 160/25/20/20): `pat_aspattern` (→34),
  `pat_aspattern_use` (→10, as-var used via `fst`), `pat_list_literal` (→6),
  `pat_list_nested` (→1020).

#### 7 — references to top-level value / mutable `Ref` bindings (A:7→0, B:254→0 genuine) — ✅ CLOSED (E1b, 2026-06-08)
The spike *used to* compute top-level **non-function** bindings as `@main`-locals;
a function body referencing one (`emitVar` → not ctor / not known-fn / not a param)
was unbound. The compiler is full of these: global mutable `Ref` state
(`typeErrors`, `curEffect`, `tjLow/tjIndex/tjStack` Tarjan state, `pendingSites`,
`recordsRef`, `outputRef`, the `argMeasureEnabled`/`emitArgStampPasses` flags…) and
point-free combinator **value** bindings (`parseExpr`, `parseLam`, `parsePipe`,
`advance`, `peekP`, …).
- **Emitter gap (structural) — CLOSED (E1b).** Each top-level non-`main` non-fn
  binding is now emitted as an LLVM **global** `@mdk_g_<name> = global i64 0`
  (`emitGlobalDecls`); an `@main` **init prologue** (`emitTopGlobals`) computes each
  rhs in **source order** and `store`s the word, then `emitVar`/`lookupVarG` lowers
  any reference to a `load i64, ptr @mdk_g_<name>` — referenceable from ANY `define`.
  A `Ref v` rhs's global holds the heap-cell POINTER, so `.value`/`set_ref` deref
  through the loaded pointer (mutation is shared across calls). A globals registry
  (`Emit`'s 10th ref, `name -> (initialised?, LTy)`) carries each binding's resolved
  static type; the `@main` body is emitted BEFORE the fn `define`s so the prologue
  resolves every LTy first. Source-order init means a forward/mutual init dependency
  (a global's rhs reading a not-yet-computed global) records a **precise** `gapE`
  instead of loading an uninitialised 0 — none occur in core.
  - Census A: 0 genuine value-global refs (core's residual `unbound x`/`acc` are
    pattern-cascade from unsupported PList/PAs heads, gap-family #4/#5, not #7).
  - Census B: the **781 → 146** "unbound variable" events (whole total **2660 →
    2025**) — all 635 removed were genuine top-level value/`Ref` references now
    lowered to global loads; the 146 residual are PRec/PList/PAs pattern-cascade
    and extern-fn-as-value, not #7.
  - Fixtures `global_value_ref` / `global_ref_mut` / `global_chain` (a value global
    read from a sibling fn, a shared mutable `Ref` global, and source-order chained
    init) are byte-identical to the oracle.
- **MULTI-MODULE init DEFERRED (D4).** This is the SINGLE-program milestone (one
  `@main` + prologue). Census B flattens core + 15 modules into ONE program, so it
  measures this capability; the REAL multi-module emit (each module's globals
  initialised in cross-module dependency order, one `@main` orchestrating them) is
  a D4 concern and is NOT built here.

#### 8 / 9 — guard residue: `otherwise` (B:88) and `__fallthrough__` (B:88) — ✅ CLOSED (E3, 2026-06-08)
`| otherwise =` references the prelude constant `otherwise = True`; guard
desugaring leaves a `__fallthrough__` sentinel name that reaches the emitter as
`CApp (CVar "__fallthrough__" AGlobal) (CLit LUnit)`.
- **`otherwise`** appears as `CVar "otherwise" AGlobal` in the condition position
  of the desugared `if`-chain (`emitVar`). **Fix**: added to `emitVar`'s constant
  arm alongside `True`/`False`/`pi`/`intMaxBound`: `x == "otherwise" → ("3", LTBool)`.
- **`__fallthrough__`** appears as `CApp (CVar "__fallthrough__" ...) (LUnit)` —
  always an application.  **Fix**: intercepted in `emitApp`'s `CVar fname _` branch
  (before `emitIndirect`) when `fname == "__fallthrough__"`: emits `call void @mdk_oob()`
  (noreturn, but NOT a terminator in LLVM IR — the surrounding `if`'s `br` terminates
  the block) and returns dummy `("0", LTInt)`.  The dead `store` + `br` the caller emits
  are unreachable but valid IR (`call noreturn` ≠ LLVM terminator; `unreachable` would
  have produced instructions-after-terminator which the IR verifier rejects).  At runtime,
  the typechecker proves guard exhaustiveness, so `__fallthrough__` is never reached;
  `mdk_oob` is a belt-and-suspenders trap matching the interpreter's `Impl_no_match` crash.
- **B: 88→0 each; core (A): 0 already** (core guard workers' residue was temporarily
  exposed by E5's let-group fix — now gone).  core total 34→**30**; whole B 3008→**2677**.
- **New fixtures** `guard_otherwise.mdk` (2-way: n>0/otherwise; main=1) and
  `guard_chain.mdk` (3-way: n>0/n==0/otherwise; main=-99); both byte-identical (149/25/20/20).

#### 10–14 — long-tail one-offs
- **non-nullary/recursive `let`-group** (5): ✅ **CLOSED (E5)** —
  `find`/`count`/`maximum`/`arbitraryList` use `where`/recursive local bindings;
  each local fn is now lambda-lifted (`emitGroupBind`, self-rec via `%clos`,
  E1a clause-tree body). Non-mutual groups thread one-at-a-time; a genuine
  forward/mutual reference is a precise `gapE` (none occurs in core). Closing it
  exposes the workers' own guard residue (#8/#9) — see the † note above the fold.
  - **forward/mutual residue — ✅ CLOSED (knot-tying, 2026-06-11; LSP gap 3/3).**
    The E5 residue (a binding forward/mutually referencing a LATER sibling, whose
    closure word isn't allocated at the capture site) is now lowered by **back-patch
    knot-tying** (`emitLetGroupKnot`).  When `refsLater` fires, the maximal leading
    run of LOCAL FUNCTION bindings is tied as a set (`emitKnotRun`): **pass 1**
    (`knotAlloc`) lifts each fn's `@mdk_lamN` define — independent of the cells,
    captures load from `%clos` fields by index — then allocs its closure cell with
    the code pointer in field 0 and PLACEHOLDER `0` capture words
    (`emitClosureAllocPatch`, returning the raw cell pointer); every sibling name is
    bound to its cell word.  **Pass 2** (`knotPatch`) stores each cell's REAL capture
    words — looked up in the fully-knotted env (siblings + enclosing scope) — into its
    capture fields via `storeFields … 1`.  The capture-NAME list per binding is
    computed against a sibling placeholder env so a sibling ref is kept by `keepInEnv`
    and indexed in the SAME field order the lifted define's `loadCaptures` reads.
    **Covered:** forward refs (`go` → later `bump`, the live `stdlib/list.mdk`
    `tally`), true mutual recursion (`isEven`/`isOdd`), and mutual recursion WITH an
    enclosing-scope capture (a sibling closing over an outer let var) — capture and
    knot-tying compose since the patch reads the joint env.  Self-recursion still
    flows through `%clos` unchanged; the nullary-VALUE fast path and the non-mutual
    single-binding path are byte-identical (the new path is taken ONLY when
    `refsLater` fires).  **Precise residual gap:** a nullary VALUE binding interleaved
    INSIDE a mutually-referencing run — a forward/mutual ref crossing a non-function
    sibling — is rejected by `anyRefsOutsideRun` rather than miscompiled (does not
    occur in the LSP graph or stdlib).  Gates held byte-identical (llvm 172, modules
    8, typed 37, build 9); fixpoint C3a/C3b YES.  **Payoff:** `medaka build
    compiler/entries/lsp_main.mdk` now SUCCEEDS — first full native compile of the LSP; all
    three LSP-blocking native emitter gaps (flushStdout, general extern-as-value,
    forward/mutual local fns) are CLOSED.
- **unbound dict witness** (`$dict_clamp_0`, `$dict_hash_0`, `$dict_when_0`,
  `$dict_unless_0`): a forwarded/nested dict use whose param isn't in the emit
  env — a dict-pass threading edge.
  - **`$dict_clamp_0` (A:2 closure events) — ✅ CLOSED (E8, 2026-06-08).**
    `clamp lo hi = min hi >> max lo` builds a closure whose body dispatches
    `min`/`max` via `RDictFwd "$dict_clamp_0"`.  `freeVars` returned `[]` for
    `CMethod`/`CDict` nodes (catch-all arm) → the dict was never added to the
    closure's capture set → `dictOperand` gapped.  **Fix**: two new arms in
    `freeVars` (before the catch-all) + helper `routeDictNames` that extracts
    `RDict`/`RDictFwd` dict-param names from a Route list; `CMethod` traverses
    all three Route fields, `CDict` traverses its routes list.  New fixture
    `dict_clamp.mdk` (oracle=10, `myClamp 0 10 99` via local lambda capturing
    Ord dict) — byte-identical (31/31 typed gate).  A: 5→3 (closure events gone;
    3 impl-body events remain — different cause).  B: 1924 unchanged (clamp was
    A-only; `when`/`unless` B-only, different cause, reported below).
  - **`$dict_debug_0`, `$dict_display_0`, `$dict_hash_0` (A:3 impl-body events)
    — ✅ CLOSED (E9, 2026-06-08).** `impl Debug (List a) requires Debug a where
    debug xs = "[\{debugListItems xs}]"` calls `debugListItems` via `EDictAt`.
    `usesImplDict`'s scanner `bodyRDictNames` → `collectMethodSites` only visited
    `EMethodAt` nodes (route ref `Ref Route`), never `EDictAt` (route LIST
    `Ref (List Route)`), so a body that FORWARDS the impl dict through a
    `=>`-constrained helper was judged not to use it → `implDictPassMethods` did
    NOT prepend the `$dict_<m>_0` param → at emit the dict was unbound.  **Fix**
    (`typecheck.mdk`): a parallel collector `collectDictSites` mirroring
    `collectMethodSites`'s structural walk EXACTLY but matching `EDictAt _ rs => [rs]`
    (+ peer site-collectors `letBindDictSites`/`armDictSites`/`guardDictSites`/
    `doStmtDictSites`/`fieldAssignDictSites`); `siteRDictNamesL`/`routeRDictName`
    extract `RDict`/`RDictFwd` names from a route list; `bodyRDictNames` unions both.
    `collectMethodSites` UNCHANGED.  **Golden-safe**: core_ir/eval/typecheck/untyped-llvm
    goldens byte-identical — on the non-emit (`implInferEnabled`/`emitArgStampPasses` OFF)
    path no EDictAt site routes RDict to an impl param, so `usesImplDict` stays False
    there (the `implDictPassMethods` comment's invariant).  New fixtures
    `dict_debug_list.mdk` (oracle=3, `debug (Cons 1 (Cons 2 (Cons 3 Nil)))` via
    `debugListItems` forwarding the element dict) and `dict_hash_list.mdk`
    (oracle=10260, `hash` length-fold) — byte-identical (33/33 typed gate).
    **A: 3→0.**  B's `debugListItems`/`displayListItems` now appear only under the
    benign slice-7 "primitive receiver carries no cell tag" arg-tag line (routing,
    gap #2), not as unbound dict witnesses.
  - **`$dict_when_0`, `$dict_unless_0` (B:2 events, A:0) — DIFFERENT ROOT CAUSE.**
    `when b m = if b then m else pure ()` emits correctly in the single-file typed
    path (A) after E8 — `pure` dispatches via `RDictFwd` from the function's own
    dict param, which IS in the env.  B-only events suggest the `elaborateModules`
    path gives `when`/`unless` a different dict-param structure.  New fixture
    `dict_when.mdk` (oracle=1, `when True (Some ())` at Option) is byte-identical
    in the single-file typed gate (31/31); the B-only regression is a separate
    investigation.
- **unit-head switch** ✅ **CLOSED (E20, 2026-06-09):** the 6 `Arbitrary` unit-head events — `arbitraryString`/`arbitrary@Int`/`arbitrary@Bool`/… — now emit. `HUnit` is irrefutable: `emitSwitch` recognises `CTBranch HUnit sub` and descends into `sub` directly without a discriminant test, dropping the focus occurrence. EMIT-ONLY (`canonPat`/Core IR/goldens untouched). Fixture `test/llvm_fixtures/unit_head.mdk`.
- **arg-tag method under-applied** (6): `fold` passed without its discriminating
  arg (first-class method value) inside `any`/`all`/`elem`.
- **non-Int literal switch** ✅ **CLOSED (E14, 2026-06-08):** String + Char literal
  match heads now lower. `emitLitChain` previously handled only `HLit (LInt k)`
  (icmp-eq against the tagged-int immediate); the 30 census-B events were the
  lexer's `keywordOrIdent`/`radixBase`/`isRadixDigit` switching on String/Char
  literals. Extension:
  - **`HLit (LChar c)`** — Char is an immediate codepoint word `(cp<<1)|1`, so the
    arm is the exact icmp-eq shape as Int (`charCode (arrayGetUnsafe 0
    (stringToChars c))` for the codepoint).
  - **`HLit (LString s)`** — String is a boxed cell; materialize the literal
    operand the same way `emitLit (LString _)` does (`@.str.N` global + `mdk_str_lit`
    box), then call the new `@mdk_string_eq(i64, i64)` runtime helper (byte_len
    compare + memcmp) which returns a TAGGED Bool word (`3`=True), and branch on
    `icmp eq i64 r, 3`. Runtime gains one helper `mdk_string_eq` in
    `runtime/medaka_rt.c` (the only contended-file exception, runtime is not
    shared).
  - **`LBool`** never reaches `emitLitChain`: `canonPat` rewrites bool literal
    patterns to `PCon "True"/"False"`, so Bool `match` is a CONSTRUCTOR-head
    switch (`emitConChain`) — see the FIXED note below.
  - Fixtures: `match_str_lit` (→2), `match_char_lit` (→2), `match_str_default`
    (wildcard default →0), all byte-identical to the tree-walker oracle.
  - **Bool match heads: ✅ FIXED (2026-06-07).** Bool `match` is a CONSTRUCTOR-head
    switch (`emitConChain`), not a literal one, but `cellTag` was not special-cased
    for `True`/`False`. `emitVar`/`emitLit` hardcode `True`→word `3` (tag 1 after
    `loadDiscriminant` ashr 1) and `False`→word `1` (tag 0), while `cellTag` returned
    0 for both (ctor not in prelude-free table → `unreachable`). Fix: `cellTag`
    special-cases `True`→`1` and `False`→`0` before the ctor-table lookup, so
    construction and discrimination agree. Gates: `match_bool_true` (→7),
    `match_bool_false` (→9), `match_bool_fn` (→300) all byte-identical; 136-fixture
    full gate still 0 failing.
  - **Float literal switch heads** (1 site in `deriveForNewtype`): remain unsupported.
    `emitLitChain` would need a float-compare path (float equality is fp `fcmp oeq`
    on the unboxed double, not a tag comparison). 1 site whole-compiler, rare — doc
    only, deferred or excluded from bootstrap scope.

---

### FRONT-END-RESIDUE vs EMITTER-GAP split

| Class | Gaps | B-count | Meaning |
|-------|------|--------:|---------|
| **Genuine emitter gap** (Core IR node/shape the emitter can't lower) | #1, #12, #14 (#3, #6, #7, #10, #11 now closed) | 1308 | needs emitter code |
| **Desugar/lower-fixable** (eliminate the shape before Core IR) | #4, #5 | 258 | ✅ CLOSED — realised emitter-side via `emitClauseTree` (E1a fn + E2b lambda); Core IR unchanged |
| **Front-end residue** (a name/sentinel that shouldn't reach emit) | #8, #9 | ~~176~~ **0** | ✅ CLOSED (E3) — `emitVar` + `emitApp` |
| **Dispatch routing, already designed** (not new work) | #2, #13 | ~~546~~ **350** | ✅ #2 PARTIAL (E4): D3b arg-pos dict-passing ported onto `elaborateModules` (gated), 1399→344; #13 (~6) remains |

---

### SCOPE READOUT — proposed closure staging

The gaps cluster into a sized sequence. Ordered by **what unblocks the most real
source per unit of work**:

- **E1 — structural prerequisites (the two big walls).** (#1 multi-clause + #7
  global value/`Ref` bindings.) Together **986** of B's events; nothing real
  emits until both land. #1 *reuses* the impl-method decision-tree lowering; #7 is
  a new top-level-globals emission pass. **Largest, highest-leverage, mostly
  emitter-work** (one reuse + one new pass).
  - **E1a (#1 multi-clause + fn-half of #4) — ✅ DONE (2026-06-07).** Multi-clause
    and patterned top-level fns route through the shared `emitClauseTree`
    decision-tree path (see gap #1 detail). core #1 16→0, whole 732→0; core total
    57→44. Re-run `llvm_emit_gaps_main.mdk` to confirm.
  - **E1b (#7 global value/`Ref` bindings) — ✅ DONE (2026-06-08).** Top-level
    non-`main` non-fn bindings emit as LLVM globals `@mdk_g_<name>`, initialised
    once in an `@main` source-order init prologue (`emitGlobalDecls` +
    `emitTopGlobals`); `emitVar`/`lookupVarG` lowers a reference to a global load
    via the new `Emit` globals registry. core #7 stays 0 (no genuine value globals;
    its residual unbound-vars are pattern-cascade); whole genuine value-global refs
    **781→146** unbound events (whole TOTAL **2660→2025**). Single-program milestone;
    multi-module cross-module init deferred to D4. Re-run `llvm_emit_gaps_main.mdk`
    to confirm.

- **E2 — value-shape lowering.** (#3/#6 `::`/`++`, #4/#5 param/lambda patterns.)
  **527** events. Half is desugar/lower (param-pattern → body `match`), half is
  small emitter cases reusing existing ctor/extern emission. Independent of E1.
  - **E2a (#3/#6 `::` and `++`) — ✅ DONE (2026-06-07).** `::` closes completely (A:2→0);
    `++` drops A:14→4 (4 remaining = unknown-param `append`/`ap` impls). New C helpers
    `mdk_string_append` / `mdk_list_append`; 4 new fixtures; core total 44→32.
  - **E2b (#4/#5 fn/lambda param patterns) — ✅ DONE (E1a fn + E2b lambda).** Both
    halves route their single clause through the shared `emitClauseTree` decision
    tree. fn (E1a): core 4→0, whole 150→0. lambda (E2b): core 1→0, whole 185→0;
    `emitPatLamDefine` lowers a patterned `CLam`/recursive-`let`-`CLam` inside the
    lifted-lambda frame. core total 32→**31**.
  - **E2c (`++` param-type residue) — ✅ DONE (2026-06-08).** Static impl-param typing
    closes `append@List` and `append@String`.  `emitGroup` now extracts `positions`
